dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses Donchian channels to express breakout logic: entry when intraday high exceeds the highest high of previous 55 days (approximated using dc_upper with 55-period Donchian), exit when intraday low falls below the lowest low of previous 20 days (approximated using dc_lower with 20-period Donchian). Note: Donchian indicators in the allowed list do not specify period, so implementation should use 55-period for entry dc_upper and 20-period for exit dc_lower. The signal mentions trader discretion allowing slightly shorter periods than 55 days—backtest engine should support parameterization of the Donchian periods.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal uses Donchian channels to express breakout logic: entry when intraday high exceeds the highest high of previous 55 days (approximated using dc_upper with 55-period Donchian), exit when intraday low falls below the lowest low of previous 20 days (approximated using dc_lower with 20-period Donchian). Note: Donchian indicators in the allowed list do not specify period, so implementation should use 55-period for entry dc_upper and 20-period for exit dc_lower. The signal mentions trader discretion allowing slightly shorter periods than 55 days—backtest engine should support parameterization of the Donchian periods.

source_rule_text: Enter a long position when the intraday high exceeds the daily highs of the previous 55 days; exit when the intraday low falls below the lowest low of the previous 20 days.
